spec_match.rationale: Page runs script and renders dynamic UI, but the visible API surface (navigator., window., @container) only partially mirrors what the feature summary calls out — the headline behaviour isn't unambiguously demonstrated.

interactive.rationale: Effectively no live controls — does not clear the interactivity bar.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Concept lede matches the feature-index promise on key terms (browser, detection, rule, supports, uses).

edge_cases.rationale: No visible edge case / fallback / error handling — only the happy path.

references.rationale: ChromeStatus link is present but the page has no dedicated `references` section — the self-heal contract is fragile.

design_system.rationale: All colours come via 27 CSS-variable references, no raw hex, no button background override without a colour override, and not a static-card pattern.

openQuestions.title: Tighten the link between the demo and the spec's headline behaviour
openQuestions.detail: Confirm the on-screen interaction maps to a concrete API call from the feature summary on chromestatus: "This feature adds an `at-rule()` function to CSS `@supports` which enables authors to feature-detect support for CSS at-rules.". If the call is being shimmed or simulated, surface that fact in the page and add a real invocation behind a feature-detect.

openQuestions.title: Add live controls and a runtime hook
openQuestions.detail: Page must offer at least a try-it button that invokes the API and shows its return value or visible side effect; current state is closer to a static card.

openQuestions.title: Add explicit edge case coverage
openQuestions.detail: Pick one failure mode the spec or chromestatus motivation calls out (unsupported browser, denied permission, empty input, etc.) and surface it as a distinct demo state alongside the happy path.

openQuestions.title: Wrap references in a labelled `references` section
openQuestions.detail: Per CLAUDE.md rule #3, the chromestatus link is the self-heal anchor and should sit inside a clearly-labelled `references` section so the recovery script can find it reliably.

openQuestions.title: Manually verify against the spec text
openQuestions.detail: Reviewer-v1-subagent based the spec_match verdict on the chromestatus summary ("This feature adds an `at-rule()` function to CSS `@supports` which enables authors to feature-detect support for CSS at-rules.") because reading the full spec for every concept was out of budget. A human or follow-up agent should diff the demo's actual behaviour against https://drafts.csswg.org/css-conditional-5/#support-definition-at-rules to catch anything chromestatus glossed over.

summary: Concept ships ~1759 chars of script across 0 button(s) and 0 other control(s). hits 2 pass / 2 partial / 2 fail across the six rubric criteria.
